workstream.name: ts2pant L2 Typed Mirror
workstream.unlocks: The IR layering principle becomes enforceable: L1→L2 is mechanical 1:1; recognizers live upstream at TS-AST→L1. Future workstreams (general-loop SSA L5 break/continue, L6 summary unification, any new recognizer family) inherit the discipline without re-discovering it. Reviews can reject L1→L2 transformations as anti-pattern by citing the discipline doc.

problemStatement: L2 (`IRExpr` in `tools/ts2pant/src/ir.ts`) reads to contributors as a transformation IR but functions as a typed builder for OpaqueExpr — the only substantive L1→L2 transformation is μ-search recognition (`isCanonicalMuSearchForm` + `lowerL1MuSearch` in `ir1-lower.ts`). The aspirational "L1 has no Pant-target awareness" framing in AGENTS.md is already broken (L1 has `IsNullish`); maintaining the fiction confuses where new recognizers should live. The recognition-at-L1→L2 placement is also a permanent footgun: future contributors adding new recognizers reach for the same wrong location.
solutionSummary: Three coordinated changes in one atomic gameplan: (1) extend L1's expression vocabulary with `comb-typed`, `forall`, `exists` to mirror L2's Pant-target forms (Comb stays L2-only); (2) migrate the μ-search recognizer to produce L1 `comb-typed` directly at the TS-AST→L1 boundary, leaving L1→L2 as a pure 1:1 mechanical mapping; (3) reframe AGENTS.md + IR doc to articulate the honest layering principle ("L1→L2 carries no recognition; recognizers live upstream") plus the corollary that L2 is not a substitution target. Snapshot equivalence on every μ-search fixture is the load-bearing correctness signal. Other Pant-target normalisations (e.g., is-nullish → cardinality) are deliberately not migrated in this gameplan; the principle is established by migrating the one load-bearing recognizer, and the rest follow as separate workstreams if a need surfaces.
currentStateAnalysis: L2 has 10 expression forms; L1 has 11 (including `is-nullish`, `map-read`, `set-read` which are L1-only normalisations). The asymmetry: L2 has `CombTyped`, `Forall`, `Exists`, `Comb` that L1 lacks. `CombTyped` is produced by μ-search lowering at `lowerL1MuSearch` (line 269+ in `ir1-lower.ts`); `Forall`/`Exists` are constructed during emission in `translate-types.ts`; `Comb` is produced by `.reduce` chain fusion at L1→L2. The μ-search recognition path today: `recognizeLetWhilePair` (TS-AST level) builds L1 `Block([Let(c, init), While(p, Assign(c, c+1))])`; `isCanonicalMuSearchForm` validates the L1 shape; `lowerL1MuSearch` rewrites to L2 `CombTyped`. Post-gameplan: `recognizeLetWhilePair`'s callers directly construct L1 `comb-typed`; `isCanonicalMuSearchForm` and `lowerL1MuSearch` are simplified/removed; L1 `comb-typed` lowers mechanically to L2 `CombTyped` via the new lowering arm. The IR1 substitution primitive (`tools/ts2pant/src/ir1-substitute.ts`, landed via PRs #231-235) exhaustively switches over `IR1Expr`; adding new variants requires new arms in every IR1Expr-exhaustive walker across the codebase (`ir1.ts`, `ir1-substitute.ts`, `ir1-lower.ts`, `ir1-printer.ts`, `ir1-ssa-counter-loop.ts`, `ir1-ssa-fixed-point.ts`). TypeScript's `_exhaustive: never` defaults make each walker a compile-time gate.

operationalConsiderations.externalSystemAccess: Not applicable. ts2pant is a build-time TypeScript translator; this gameplan only modifies internal IR modules and one TS-AST recognizer. No SDK / network / IAM changes.
operationalConsiderations.crossRuntimeContracts: The TS-side ↔ wasm-side boundary (via `ir-emit.ts` → `ast.*`) is unchanged — the migration produces structurally-identical L2 output that emits to the same OpaqueExpr shapes. The implicit ts2pant→Pant contract (the Pant source ts2pant emits) is gated by snapshot equivalence on every fixture; any drift aborts. No other runtime consumes ts2pant's output.
operationalConsiderations.failureBehavior: Three failure paths. (a) Snapshot drift: if any fixture in `tools/ts2pant/tests/constructs.test.mts.snapshot` changes byte-wise after Patch 3 lands, the migration has altered behavior and is a stop-the-line condition — the implementing agent does not regenerate snapshots; they raise the issue. (b) TypeScript compile errors after Patch 2: adding new IR1Expr variants surfaces every exhaustive walker that needs new arms (the `_exhaustive: never` defaults). Patch 2 must update all of them in the same patch so `tsc` succeeds before the patch lands. (c) `CaptureRiskError` from the IR1 substitution primitive: future callers that substitute under the new L1 binder forms (none exist in this gameplan) would observe correct capture-avoidance behaviour via the new arms in `ir1-substitute.ts`.
operationalConsiderations.concurrencyAndIdempotency: Not applicable. ts2pant is single-threaded; the new IR1 constructors and walker arms are pure functions over ADT values. No shared state.
operationalConsiderations.rollbackStrategy: Forward-only. The gameplan is additive in Patch 2 (new types, constructors, walker arms), behaviour-preserving in Patch 3 (logic moves from `ir1-lower.ts` to TS-AST recognizers; snapshot-equivalent), and additive in Patch 4 (docs). `git revert` of the patches in reverse order restores the pre-refactor state. Snapshot tests gate every step.

mergabilityStrategy.featureFlagStrategy: No feature flag. Patch 2 adds dormant L1 vocabulary (no production caller). Patch 3's migration is snapshot-equivalent — observable output is byte-identical before and after. Patch 4 is text-only. Toggling at runtime serves no purpose; either the migration is correct (snapshot tests pass) or it is wrong (snapshot tests fail and the gameplan aborts).
mergabilityStrategy.patchOrderingStrategy: Sequential, four patches. Patch 1 (INFRA): pending unit / property / migration tests. Patch 2 (INFRA): L1 vocabulary expansion with arms in every IR1Expr-exhaustive walker; un-skip the vocabulary tests. Patch 3 (INFRA): μ-search recognizer migration — `recognizeLetWhilePair`'s callers build L1 `comb-typed` directly; `lowerL1MuSearch` / `isCanonicalMuSearchForm` are simplified or removed; snapshot-equivalent on every μ-search fixture; un-skip the migration tests. Patch 4 (INFRA): documentation — IR doc layering-principle restatement, AGENTS.md framing update, imperative-IR workstream cross-reference correction.

requiredChanges.description: Create (or extend) with `it.skip(...)` stubs for the three new L1 constructors and their structural-equality behaviour. PENDING Patch 2.

Add `it.skip(...)` stubs for the new binder forms: free-vars scope on `comb-typed` / `forall` / `exists`; capture-risk rejection under their binders; shadow-needle halt; non-shadowed recursion. Extend the property-test `fc.letrec` generator to include the new forms (also skipped pending Patch 2).

Add `it.skip(...)` stubs for the mechanical 1:1 lowering: L1 `comb-typed` → L2 `CombTyped` via `irCombTyped`; L1 `forall` → L2 `Forall` via `irForall`; L1 `exists` → L2 `Exists` via `irExists` (with and without guard). PENDING Patch 2.

Add `it.skip(...)` stubs for the migration: `recognizeLetWhilePair` (or its successor) builds L1 `comb-typed` directly; μ-search fixtures produce byte-identical Pant output; the L1→L2 lowering arm for `comb-typed` is the only path that emits L2 `CombTyped` for μ-search. PENDING Patch 3.

Extend `IR1Expr` union with three new variants: `{ kind: "comb-typed"; combiner: Combiner; binder: string; binderType: string; guards: IR1Expr[]; proj: IR1Expr }`; `{ kind: "forall"; binder: string; binderType: string; guard?: IR1Expr; body: IR1Expr }`; `{ kind: "exists"; binder: string; binderType: string; guard?: IR1Expr; body: IR1Expr }`. Add constructor helpers `ir1CombTyped`, `ir1Forall`, `ir1Exists`. Extend `ir1SsaExprEquals` with three arms.
requiredChanges.signature:
export const ir1CombTyped = (combiner: Combiner, binder: string, binderType: string, guards: IR1Expr[], proj: IR1Expr): IR1Expr
export const ir1Forall = (binder: string, binderType: string, body: IR1Expr, guard?: IR1Expr): IR1Expr
export const ir1Exists = (binder: string, binderType: string, body: IR1Expr, guard?: IR1Expr): IR1Expr

requiredChanges.description: Add three new arms to `freeVarsIR1Expr` (each scopes its binder out of guards/proj or body) and three new arms to the internal `substituteExpr` walker (each applies `throwIfCaptureRisk` on its binder, halts when the binder shadows the needle, otherwise recurses). Mirror the existing `each` arm's structure.

Add three new arms to `lowerL1Expr` (1:1 mechanical mappings via `irCombTyped` / `irForall` / `irExists`). After Patch 3 lands, remove or simplify `lowerL1MuSearch` and `isCanonicalMuSearchForm` — the L1→L2 dispatch for μ-search is now just the `comb-typed` arm.
Add three new arms for human-readable debug output of the new forms. Required for exhaustive `_exhaustive: never` checks.
Add new arms to every IR1Expr-exhaustive walker in this file (lines 652, 722, 822). Recursive scope-aware traversal patterns; required for `tsc` exhaustiveness.
Add new arms to every IR1Expr-exhaustive walker in this file (lines 577, 652, 729, 817, 886). Same pattern as `ir1-ssa-counter-loop.ts`.

Migrate `recognizeLetWhilePair` (or its callers) to build L1 `comb-typed` directly when the let-while pair matches the canonical μ-search shape. The recognition logic that currently lives at `isCanonicalMuSearchForm` (`ir1-lower.ts`) moves here. The TS-AST recognizer becomes the canonical home for μ-search recognition; L1 production is direct (no Block([Let, While]) intermediate that requires a later canonical-form check).
If `recognizeLetWhilePair` is called from here too, ensure the L1 `comb-typed` construction is shared with `translate-body.ts`. Otherwise no change.

Remove or substantially simplify `lowerL1MuSearch` and `isCanonicalMuSearchForm`. After Patch 3, the only L1→L2 dispatch needed for μ-search is the mechanical `comb-typed` → `CombTyped` arm added in Patch 2. If `lowerL1MuSearch` is retained as a helper, ensure no recognition logic remains in it — it must be a pure structural pass-through.

Rewrite the "Layering principle" subsection per the workstream's M3 (now part of this single milestone): replace the "L1 has no Pant-target awareness" framing with the honest "L1 is the canonical form after all source-level recognition; L2 is L1's expression subset packaged as a typed OpaqueExpr mirror." Add the discipline statement (L1→L2 carries no recognition) and the corollary (L2 is not a substitution target).

Update Foundational Pattern / IRSC references to note ts2pant's divergence from the original IRSC purpose (no L2-reading verifier; separation is for type safety and inspectability only). Cross-reference the discipline statement now in the IR doc.
Where the imperative-IR workstream asserts L1 has "no Pant-target awareness," correct to align with the post-refactor framing.

functionalChanges.description: μ-search recognition logic moves from the L1→L2 boundary (`isCanonicalMuSearchForm` + `lowerL1MuSearch` in `ir1-lower.ts`) to the TS-AST → L1 boundary (`recognizeLetWhilePair` and its callers in `translate-body.ts` / `ir1-build.ts`). The TS-AST recognizer constructs L1 `comb-typed` directly; the L1→L2 lowering for μ-search becomes a pure 1:1 mechanical mapping via the `comb-typed` arm. Existing μ-search fixtures produce byte-identical Pant output (snapshot-equivalent).

contextResources.why: Authoritative source for the milestone's Definition of Done: which forms to add, the recognizer migration scope, the documentation restatement, the snapshot-equivalence gate, and the locked decisions (no Comb at L1, no other normalisation migrations, no lint enforcement). Every patch's implementing agent reads this for context.

L2's `CombTyped` (line ~182), `Forall` (line ~192), `Exists` (line ~202) definitions are the shapes L1 must mirror. L2 uses `binderType: string` uniformly; the constructors `irCombTyped` (line 343), `irForall` (line 358), `irExists` (line 368) define the API the L1 → L2 lowering arms call. Patch 2's implementing agent confirms field names and constructor signatures match exactly.

The IR1 substitution primitive's `each` arm (line 314 in `substituteExpr`, line 66 in `freeVarsIR1Expr`) is the structural template for the new binder-introducing forms. The helpers (`union`, `addName`, `without`, `throwIfCaptureRisk`, `shadowsNeedle`) are reused unchanged. Patch 2 extends both walkers with three new arms each. Patch 3 consumes `substituteIR1ExprSubtree` directly: the recognizer migration replaces the counter variable with the fresh comprehension binder inside the negated predicate, which is exactly what the primitive does (Var-needle substitution at L1).

The existing L1 `each` form (definition around line 118; constructor `ir1Each` around line 1094; `ir1SsaExprEquals` arm around line 928) is the structural template for the new binder-introducing forms. `comb-typed` mirrors `each` minus the `src` field; `forall` and `exists` mirror `each` minus `src` plus optional `guard`.

Every file in this list contains at least one switch over `IR1Expr` whose `default` branch asserts `const _exhaustive: never = expr`. Adding new IR1Expr variants produces TypeScript compile errors in each. The implementing agent uses `grep -n 'exhaustive: never = ' tools/ts2pant/src/*.ts` to enumerate the walkers, reads each to understand its purpose, and adds new arms following each walker's idiom.

The current μ-search recognition path is the predecessor Patch 3 migrates. `recognizeLetWhilePair` (in translate-body.ts) builds L1 `Block([Let, While(_, Assign)])`; `isCanonicalMuSearchForm` (in ir1-lower.ts, line ~159+) validates the L1 shape; `lowerL1MuSearch` (in ir1-lower.ts, line ~269+) rewrites to L2 `CombTyped`. After Patch 3, the recognition + canonical-form check + counter-binder substitution all live at the TS-AST → L1 boundary, producing L1 `comb-typed` directly. The implementing agent must preserve every aspect of the recognizer's existing semantics (counter detection, predicate validation, step verification, binder allocation via `cellRegisterName`, NumericStrategy gating).

Snapshot equivalence is Patch 3's gate. The μ-search fixture exercises every supported spelling (`i++`, `++i`, `i += 1`, `i = i + 1`, `i = 1 + i` plus the long-form sites in `name-registry.ts` and `translate-signature.ts` via dogfood). The snapshot must remain byte-identical after the recognizer migration; any drift indicates the migration has changed behavior and is stop-the-line. The existing M2 test file (`m2-musearch-l1.test.mts`) is the regression suite for the L1-level μ-search canonical form; Patch 3 extends it to assert the recognizer now produces L1 `comb-typed` directly.

The existing fast-check `arbIR1Expr` generator at line 293 uses `fc.letrec` to randomly construct IR1Expr instances for property-based testing. Patch 2's extension adds the three new forms to this generator's `fc.oneof` so property tests exercise capture-risk and shadowing behaviour on them.

The target doc for Patch 4's layering-principle restatement and the L1 vocabulary note. The existing "Layering principle" subsection (currently around the `## IRExpr — 10 forms` table) is rewritten; the new statement reads: "L1 is the canonical form after all source-level recognition; L2 is L1's expression subset packaged as a typed OpaqueExpr mirror. All recognition and target-shape decisions happen at TS-AST → L1; L1→L2 is a mechanical 1:1 lowering."

Patch 4 updates AGENTS.md's Foundational Pattern / IRSC references to note ts2pant's divergence from the original IRSC purpose. The existing reference frames IRSC as ts2pant's foundational precedent without flagging the difference (IRSC has a downstream verifier reading the SSA-pure IR; ts2pant does not). The updated framing acknowledges the divergence honestly.

The imperative-IR workstream asserts L1 has "no Pant-target awareness" — a claim that was already aspirational (L1 has IsNullish) and is sharpened by this gameplan's recognizer migration. Patch 4 corrects the assertion to align with the post-refactor framing.

acceptanceCriteria: `tools/ts2pant/src/ir1.ts` exports three new IR1Expr variants (`comb-typed`, `forall`, `exists`) and constructors (`ir1CombTyped`, `ir1Forall`, `ir1Exists`).
`ir1SsaExprEquals` and every other IR1Expr-exhaustive walker in the codebase has new arms for each new form; `tsc --noEmit` succeeds.
`freeVarsIR1Expr` in `ir1-substitute.ts` correctly scopes each new form's binder out of guards/proj/body; capture-risk and shadow-needle handling matches the existing `each` arm.
`lowerL1Expr` in `ir1-lower.ts` has mechanical 1:1 arms mapping each new L1 form to its L2 counterpart.

The μ-search recognizer (`recognizeLetWhilePair` and callers in `translate-body.ts` / `ir1-build.ts`) constructs L1 `comb-typed` directly when the let-while pair matches the canonical shape; `lowerL1MuSearch` and `isCanonicalMuSearchForm` are removed or simplified to pure structural pass-through.
`tools/ts2pant/tests/constructs.test.mts.snapshot` is byte-identical before and after Patch 3 (snapshot-equivalence on every μ-search fixture). Any drift aborts the gameplan.
Unit tests cover construction, structural equality, free-vars scope, substitution capture-rejection + shadowing, and 1:1 lowering for each new L1 form.
Migration tests confirm `recognizeLetWhilePair` builds L1 `comb-typed` directly; existing M2 μ-search test (`m2-musearch-l1.test.mts`) passes against the new L1 shape.

`tools/ts2pant/docs/intermediate-representation.md`'s "Layering principle" is rewritten honestly; discipline statement (no L1→L2 recognition) and corollary (L2 is not a substitution target) land in the IR doc.
`tools/ts2pant/AGENTS.md`'s IRSC references note ts2pant's divergence from the original purpose.
`workstreams/ts2pant-imperative-ir.md` is corrected where it asserts L1 has no Pant-target awareness.
Full ts2pant test suite (`just ts2pant-test` + `just ts2pant-test-integration`) passes; `pant --check` results are identical to pre-refactor for every μ-search fixture.

explicitOpinions.opinion: Mirror L2's `binderType: string` field exactly on the new L1 forms (no separate `IR1TypeExpr` ADT).
explicitOpinions.rationale: L2's `irCombTyped`, `irForall`, `irExists` constructors all take `binderType: string` (textual type name like `"Nat0"`, `"Int"`, `"Real"`). L1's mirror uses the same shape; the L1 → L2 lowering arm is a direct pass-through. A structured `IR1TypeExpr` ADT would be speculative future-proofing for a generalisation L2 has not made.

`Comb` is produced exclusively by `.reduce` chain fusion at the L1→L2 boundary; no recognizer in this workstream migrates chain fusion. Adding `Comb` to L1 with no upstream caller is dormant code with no near-term consumer. If a future workstream migrates chain fusion upstream, `Comb` joins L1 at the same time.

explicitOpinions.opinion: Field name `binderType` (matching L2), not `type` (as the original workstream prose suggested).
explicitOpinions.rationale: L2's existing constructors use `binderType` uniformly. Using a different name at L1 would force the lowering arm to do field renaming, complicating the "mechanical 1:1" claim.

explicitOpinions.opinion: Single milestone / single gameplan, not three milestones (vocabulary + migration + docs).
explicitOpinions.rationale: An earlier draft of the workstream split this into M1 (vocabulary), M2 (migration), M3 (docs). On re-examination against the gameplan atomicity criteria, no human intervention, observation window, or outcome-conditional decision sits between any pair. The "M1 stands alone if M2 reveals problems" framing was self-fulfilling — snapshot tests catch issues in the migration patch regardless of milestone boundary. One atomic gameplan with `dependsOn` between patches is the right structure. Same correction as the substitution workstream's collapse.

explicitOpinions.opinion: Bundle all three L1 form additions into Patch 2, not per-form patches.
explicitOpinions.rationale: All three forms touch the same files (`ir1.ts`, `ir1-substitute.ts`, `ir1-lower.ts`, `ir1-printer.ts`, two ssa-loop walkers). Per-form patches would either serialise (no parallelism benefit) or conflict on every file (no review isolation gain). The three forms are structurally identical — each follows the existing `each` arm's pattern — so reviewing them together is no harder.

explicitOpinions.opinion: Defer non-μ-search L1→L2 normalisation migrations to separate workstreams.
explicitOpinions.rationale: Other Pant-target normalisations exist at L1→L2 (e.g., is-nullish → cardinality `#x = 0` shape via mechanical lower). The workstream's M2 open question asked whether to fold these in; the default is defer. The principle is established by migrating the load-bearing recognizer (μ-search); the rest can follow as separate workstreams if a need surfaces. Bundling would scope-creep this gameplan.

explicitOpinions.opinion: Snapshot equivalence is the load-bearing correctness gate for Patch 3.
explicitOpinions.rationale: Patch 3 is behavior-preserving by construction — the recognizer's logic moves from one location to another but produces identical Pant output. Snapshot tests at the byte level are the highest-precision signal of preservation; any drift indicates the migration has changed semantics and is stop-the-line. The implementing agent does not regenerate snapshots on drift; they raise the issue.

explicitOpinions.opinion: Discipline by documentation + review, not lint enforcement.
explicitOpinions.rationale: Patch 4 lands the discipline statement in the IR doc + AGENTS.md. Review applies it. A lint rule (e.g., ESLint catching `lowerL1*Recognizer`-style function names in `ir1-lower.ts`) is over-investment until a violation has been attempted. If violations recur, lint can land in a follow-on.

patches.title: Pending tests for L1 vocabulary and μ-search migration

files.description: If not present, create. Add `it.skip(...)` stubs for the new L1 constructors and their structural-equality behaviour. PENDING Patch 2.
Add `it.skip(...)` stubs for free-vars scope / capture-risk / shadow-halt on each new L1 binder form. Add the property-test generator extension as a skipped stub. PENDING Patch 2.
Add `it.skip(...)` stubs for the mechanical 1:1 lowering arms. PENDING Patch 2.

Add `it.skip(...)` stubs for the migration assertions: `recognizeLetWhilePair` produces L1 `comb-typed` directly; the canonical L1 form post-recognizer is `comb-typed` (not Block([Let, While])); existing μ-search fixtures snapshot-equivalent. PENDING Patch 3.

changes: Create `tools/ts2pant/tests/ir1.test.mts` if it doesn't exist; otherwise extend with a new `describe('new L1 binder forms', () => { ... })` block containing skipped stubs for `ir1CombTyped` / `ir1Forall` / `ir1Exists` constructor shape and `ir1SsaExprEquals` per form.

In `tools/ts2pant/tests/ir1-substitute.test.mts`, add skipped stubs in the existing `describe('unit', ...)` block: free-vars scope for each new form; capture-risk rejection under each new binder; shadow-needle halt.

In `tools/ts2pant/tests/ir1-lower.test.mts`, add skipped stubs in a new or existing `describe('lowering new L1 forms', ...)` block: L1 → L2 mechanical lowering for each new form (with and without guard for forall/exists).

In `tools/ts2pant/tests/m2-musearch-l1.test.mts`, add skipped stubs in a new `describe('post-migration recognizer placement', ...)` block: `recognizeLetWhilePair` returns L1 `comb-typed` directly; μ-search snapshot-equivalence assertion for each fixture variant.
Each stub body contains only the PENDING comment naming the implementing patch — no references to symbols that don't exist yet.
Run `npm run test:unit` and confirm the files compile and stubs are reported as skipped.

ir1 > new L1 binder forms > ir1SsaExprEquals compares forall fields

ir1-substitute > unit > substituteIR1ExprSubtree halts at shadowing forall

patches.spec:
module TS2PANT_L2_TYPED_MIRROR_PATCH_1.

> Patch 1 lands the test scaffolding for the workstream.
> Stubs are skipped; Patches 2 and 3 unskip them with
> concrete assertions. No source behavior changes; the test
> files compile and report stubs as skipped.

TestStub.
PendingFor.
patch-2 => PendingFor.
patch-3 => PendingFor.

stub-pending-for s: TestStub => PendingFor.
stub-skipped? s: TestStub => Bool.
---
all s: TestStub | stub-skipped? s.

patches.title: Add comb-typed / forall / exists L1 forms; extend every IR1Expr walker

files.description: Extend `IR1Expr` union with three new variants. Add constructors. Extend `ir1SsaExprEquals` with three arms.
Extend `freeVarsIR1Expr` and the internal `substituteExpr` walker with three arms each. Mirror the existing `each` arm's structure.
Extend `lowerL1Expr` with three 1:1 mechanical lowering arms: L1 → L2 via `irCombTyped` / `irForall` / `irExists`.
Extend the IR1 printer with arms for the three new forms (debug-readable output).
Extend every IR1Expr-exhaustive walker in this file (lines 652, 722, 822) with three new arms following each walker's idiom.
Extend every IR1Expr-exhaustive walker in this file (lines 577, 652, 729, 817, 886) with three new arms following each walker's idiom.
Unskip Patch-2-owned stubs; implement concrete assertions.
Unskip Patch-2-owned stubs; extend the `arbIR1Expr` property-test generator to produce the new forms; implement assertions.
Unskip Patch-2-owned lowering-test stubs; implement assertions.

changes: In `ir1.ts`, extend the `IR1Expr` union with three new discriminated-union members: `{ kind: "comb-typed"; combiner: Combiner; binder: string; binderType: string; guards: IR1Expr[]; proj: IR1Expr }`; `{ kind: "forall"; binder: string; binderType: string; guard?: IR1Expr; body: IR1Expr }`; `{ kind: "exists"; binder: string; binderType: string; guard?: IR1Expr; body: IR1Expr }`. Match L2's `irForall` / `irExists` / `irCombTyped` constructor signatures exactly.
Add constructors `ir1CombTyped`, `ir1Forall`, `ir1Exists` matching L2's argument order.
Extend `ir1SsaExprEquals` with three arms. Each compares fields recursively; `binderType` is a string compare; `guards` uses the existing `ir1SsaArrayEquals(a.guards, b.guards, ir1SsaExprEquals)`.

In `ir1-substitute.ts`, extend `freeVarsIR1Expr` with arms. `comb-typed`: `union(...e.guards.map(freeVarsIR1Expr), freeVarsIR1Expr(e.proj))`, then `scoped.delete(e.binder)`, return. `forall` / `exists`: same pattern with optional `guard` + `body` instead.

In `ir1-substitute.ts`, extend the internal `substituteExpr` walker with arms. Each calls `throwIfCaptureRisk(e.binder, replacementFreeVars)`. If `shadowsNeedle(e.binder, needle)`, return `expr` unchanged. Otherwise recurse and reconstruct via the new constructor.

In `ir1-lower.ts`, extend `lowerL1Expr` with three arms before `default`: `case "comb-typed": return irCombTyped(e.combiner, e.binder, e.binderType, e.guards.map(lowerL1Expr), lowerL1Expr(e.proj));` and equivalent for `forall` / `exists`.
In `ir1-printer.ts`, extend the printer with three arms (match the existing `each` printer style).

In `ir1-ssa-counter-loop.ts` and `ir1-ssa-fixed-point.ts`, identify every IR1Expr-exhaustive walker via `grep -n 'exhaustive: never = expr'` (and similar). Read each walker's purpose; add new arms following each idiom (recursive scope-aware traversal for free-vars / count-reads / equality walkers).
Verify `npx tsc --noEmit` succeeds from `tools/ts2pant/` with zero errors.

In the test files, unskip Patch-2-owned stubs and replace PENDING comments with concrete assertions. For lowering tests, construct an L1 form, call `lowerL1Expr`, assert the result equals the L2 counterpart via `irCombTyped` / `irForall` / `irExists`.
Extend the `arbIR1Expr` generator in `tests/ir1-substitute.test.mts` (line 293) to produce the three new forms via `fc.tuple(...).map(...)` mirroring the existing `each` generator.
Run `just ts2pant-test` to confirm all tests pass and existing fixtures' snapshots remain byte-identical.

precedents.name: Discriminated-union extension with exhaustive switch checks
precedents.whyApplicable: TypeScript's `const _exhaustive: never = expr;` idiom at every switch's default branch surfaces every walker over `IR1Expr` that needs new arms when the union grows. Once `tsc` succeeds with no errors after adding all arms, the implementing agent has proof that every walker is handled.

patches.spec:
module TS2PANT_L2_TYPED_MIRROR_PATCH_2.

> Patch 2 extends L1 with three new binder-introducing
> forms mirroring L2. Each form has a constructor, an arm
> in every IR1Expr-exhaustive walker (structural equality,
> free-vars scope, substitution with capture-check and
> shadow-halt, mechanical L1→L2 lowering, debug printer,
> ssa-loop walkers). The new vocabulary is consumed by
> Patch 3's recognizer migration.

IR1Form.
L2Form.
Walker.
FreeVarSet.
comb-typed => IR1Form.
forall => IR1Form.
exists => IR1Form.
comb-typed-l2 => L2Form.
forall-l2 => L2Form.
exists-l2 => L2Form.
free-vars-walker => Walker.
substitute-walker => Walker.
equality-walker => Walker.
lowering-walker => Walker.
printer-walker => Walker.
ssa-counter-walker => Walker.
ssa-fixed-point-walker => Walker.

has-arm-for? w: Walker, f: IR1Form => Bool.
scopes-binder? f: IR1Form, vars: FreeVarSet => Bool.
lowers-to? l1: IR1Form, l2: L2Form => Bool.
respects-capture-risk? f: IR1Form => Bool.
halts-at-shadow? f: IR1Form => Bool.
---

> Every IR1Expr-exhaustive walker has arms for each new form.
all f: IR1Form, w: Walker | has-arm-for? w f.

> Free-vars walker scopes binders correctly.
all f: IR1Form, vars: FreeVarSet | scopes-binder? f vars.

> Substitution arms respect capture-risk and shadowing.
all f: IR1Form | respects-capture-risk? f.
all f: IR1Form | halts-at-shadow? f.

> L1 → L2 lowering arms are 1:1 mechanical.
lowers-to? comb-typed comb-typed-l2.
lowers-to? forall forall-l2.
lowers-to? exists exists-l2.

patches.title: Migrate μ-search recognizer to produce L1 comb-typed directly

files.description: Migrate `recognizeLetWhilePair` (and its callers) to construct L1 `comb-typed` directly when the let-while pair matches the canonical μ-search shape. The recognizer absorbs the canonical-form check logic that previously lived at `isCanonicalMuSearchForm` (`ir1-lower.ts`).
If `recognizeLetWhilePair` has additional construction call sites in this file, ensure the L1 `comb-typed` construction is shared.

Remove `lowerL1MuSearch` and `isCanonicalMuSearchForm`. The L1→L2 dispatch for μ-search is now just the mechanical `comb-typed` → `CombTyped` arm added in Patch 2. If callers reference `lowerL1MuSearch` elsewhere, update them to call `lowerL1Expr` directly.

Unskip Patch-3-owned stubs. Update existing tests that asserted the post-recognizer L1 shape was `Block([Let, While])` — the new shape is `comb-typed`. Add assertions confirming the recognizer produces L1 `comb-typed` directly.

MUST be byte-identical after this patch. If `just ts2pant-test-update-snapshots` produces a diff for any μ-search fixture (`expressions-while-mu-search.ts`), the migration has changed behavior and is a STOP — do not commit the diff.

changes: Read the existing `recognizeLetWhilePair` in `translate-body.ts` to understand its current output (L1 `Block([Let, While(_, Assign)])`) and its caller context.

Read `isCanonicalMuSearchForm` in `ir1-lower.ts` (line ~159+) to identify the validation checks it performs (counter identification, step-is-`+1` check, predicate-references-counter check, side-effect screen).

Read `lowerL1MuSearch` in `ir1-lower.ts` (line ~269+) to identify the construction it performs (fresh binder allocation via `cellRegisterName`, predicate rewriting with counter→binder substitution, guard list construction, NumericStrategy-based binder-type selection, L2 `CombTyped` assembly).

Migrate the recognition and construction logic from `ir1-lower.ts` to `translate-body.ts` (or `ir1-build.ts` if that's where construction site lives). The TS-AST recognizer should: detect the let-while pair structurally; validate the canonical shape (counter, step, predicate); allocate the fresh binder; build the negated predicate with the binder substituted for the counter; construct L1 `comb-typed` via `ir1CombTyped("min", binder, binderType, [lowerBoundGuard, negatedPredicate], ir1Var(binder))`.

Use `substituteIR1ExprSubtree` from `ir1-substitute.ts` for the counter→binder substitution inside the predicate (this is the natural caller for the primitive — replaces the inline rewriting that previously lived in `lowerL1MuSearch`).
Remove `lowerL1MuSearch` and `isCanonicalMuSearchForm` from `ir1-lower.ts`. The L1→L2 dispatch for `comb-typed` is now just the mechanical arm added in Patch 2.
If any caller previously invoked `lowerL1MuSearch` directly, update it to invoke `lowerL1Expr` on the now-canonical L1 `comb-typed` form.

Unskip Patch-3-owned stubs in `tools/ts2pant/tests/m2-musearch-l1.test.mts`. Update any existing assertions that previously checked for L1 `Block([Let, While])` shape — the canonical post-recognizer form is now L1 `comb-typed`.
Run `just ts2pant-test` to confirm all tests pass.
Run `just ts2pant-test-integration` to confirm `pant --check` results are identical.

Inspect the constructs snapshot diff via `git diff tools/ts2pant/tests/constructs.test.mts.snapshot`. **Expect empty diff.** If non-empty, abort the patch and investigate the cause; do not commit the snapshot regeneration.

precedents.name: Recognizer-at-source / canonical-IR pattern
precedents.whyApplicable: Source-level recognition with structural IR target — every IR layer below the source AST is mechanical, with all decisions firing at the source-AST → canonical-IR boundary. This patch is the canonical instance for ts2pant's μ-search recognizer. Read the workstream doc's `Established Precedents` section for the pattern's broader literature.

testStubsImplemented: m2-musearch-l1 > post-migration recognizer placement > recognizeLetWhilePair produces L1 comb-typed
m2-musearch-l1 > post-migration recognizer placement > μ-search fixtures snapshot-equivalent
patches.spec:
module TS2PANT_L2_TYPED_MIRROR_PATCH_3.

> Patch 3 migrates μ-search recognition from L1→L2
> (lowerL1MuSearch + isCanonicalMuSearchForm) upstream to
> TS-AST → L1 (recognizeLetWhilePair and callers). The TS-AST
> recognizer constructs L1 comb-typed directly; the L1→L2
> dispatch becomes a pure 1:1 mechanical mapping via Patch 2's
> comb-typed arm. Snapshot equivalence on every μ-search
> fixture gates the patch.

RecognitionSite.
IR1Shape.
SnapshotState.
ts-ast-to-l1 => RecognitionSite.
l1-to-l2 => RecognitionSite.
comb-typed-l1 => IR1Shape.
block-let-while => IR1Shape.
identical => SnapshotState.
drifted => SnapshotState.

recognizer-fires-at? site: RecognitionSite => Bool.
post-recognizer-shape => IR1Shape.
snapshot-state => SnapshotState.
lower-l1-musearch-exists? => Bool.
is-canonical-musearch-form-exists? => Bool.
---

> Recognition moves upstream.
recognizer-fires-at? ts-ast-to-l1.
~(recognizer-fires-at? l1-to-l2).

> The L1 form post-recognizer is comb-typed (not Block-Let-While).
post-recognizer-shape = comb-typed-l1.

> The old L1→L2 recognition functions are removed.
~(lower-l1-musearch-exists?).
~(is-canonical-musearch-form-exists?).

> Snapshot equivalence gates the patch.
snapshot-state = identical.

patches.title: Reframe the IR layering principle in docs

files.description: Rewrite the "Layering principle" subsection: replace aspirational "L1 has no Pant-target awareness" with honest "L1 is the canonical form after all source-level recognition; L2 is L1's expression subset packaged as a typed OpaqueExpr mirror. All recognition and target-shape decisions happen at TS-AST → L1; L1→L2 is a mechanical 1:1 lowering." Add the discipline statement (no L1→L2 recognition) and the corollary (L2 is not a substitution target).
Update Foundational Pattern / IRSC references to note ts2pant's divergence from the original IRSC purpose (no L2-reading verifier; the separation is for type safety and inspectability only).
Correct the assertion that L1 has "no Pant-target awareness" to align with the post-refactor framing.

changes: In `tools/ts2pant/docs/intermediate-representation.md`, locate the existing "Layering principle" framing (currently around `## IRExpr — 10 forms` or in an adjacent section). Replace the aspirational "L1 — canonicalized TypeScript syntax. No Pantagruel-target awareness" with the honest statement: "L1 is the canonical form after all source-level recognition. L2 is L1's expression subset packaged as a typed OpaqueExpr mirror; its purpose is type-safe construction and snapshot-testable inspection, not transformation. All recognition and target-shape decisions happen at TS-AST → L1; L1→L2 is a mechanical 1:1 lowering."

Add a discipline statement in a clearly-labelled subsection: "L1→L2 carries no recognition decisions. New recognizers must fire at TS-AST → L1 and produce canonical L1 forms (e.g., `comb-typed`). Review will reject any L1→L2 transformation that is not a 1:1 mechanical mapping or a structural rewrite of an already-canonical L1 form."

Add the corollary statement: "L2 is not a substitution target. Any transformation that wants to rewrite an L2 expression is asking the wrong question — either build the canonical L1 form upstream and substitute there (using the IR1 substitution primitive from `workstreams/ts2pant-ir1-substitution.md`), or lower to OpaqueExpr and use `ast.substituteBinder`. The typed-mirror principle has no exceptions; substitution-disguised-as-construction is still a transformation."

In `tools/ts2pant/AGENTS.md`, locate the Foundational Pattern / IRSC reference. Add a note acknowledging ts2pant's divergence from IRSC: "IRSC's separation between FRSC (TS-shape) and IRSC (SSA-pure) serves a verification-soundness purpose because IRSC has a downstream consumer (the refinement-type checker). ts2pant has no L2-reading verifier; the FRSC/IRSC-style separation here is for type safety and inspectability only. L2 is a typed mirror of OpaqueExpr, not a transformation IR."

In `workstreams/ts2pant-imperative-ir.md`, find any assertion that L1 has "no Pant-target awareness." Update to align with the post-refactor framing — L1 absorbs Pant-target forms from upstream recognition; L1→L2 is mechanical.
Confirm the additions read clearly to a reviewer unfamiliar with the workstream's history; the discipline is only useful if future contributors discover it.
patches.spec:
module TS2PANT_L2_TYPED_MIRROR_PATCH_4.

> Patch 4 reframes the IR layering principle in docs. The
> IR doc, AGENTS.md, and the imperative-IR workstream doc
> are updated to articulate the honest layering: L1 absorbs
> Pant-target forms; L1→L2 is mechanical; L2 is a typed
> mirror, not a transformation IR.

Document.
DocumentKind.
ir-doc => DocumentKind.
agents-md => DocumentKind.
imperative-ir-doc => DocumentKind.

document-kind d: Document => DocumentKind.
mentions-layering-principle? d: Document => Bool.
mentions-no-l1-l2-recognition? d: Document => Bool.
mentions-l2-not-substitution-target? d: Document => Bool.
---

all d: Document |
  document-kind d = ir-doc ->
    mentions-layering-principle? d and
    mentions-no-l1-l2-recognition? d and
    mentions-l2-not-substitution-target? d.

all d: Document |
  document-kind d = agents-md ->
    mentions-layering-principle? d.

all d: Document |
  document-kind d = imperative-ir-doc ->
    mentions-layering-principle? d.

mergabilityInsight: All 4 patches are INFRA. Patch 1 lands pending tests. Patch 2 adds the new L1 vocabulary and updates every IR1Expr-exhaustive walker (typescript's `_exhaustive: never` defaults gate the work). Patch 3 migrates the μ-search recognizer to produce L1 `comb-typed` directly; snapshot-equivalence on every μ-search fixture is the load-bearing correctness signal. Patch 4 reframes the IR doc + AGENTS.md + imperative-IR workstream doc to articulate the honest layering. No BEHAVIOR patches because the migration is byte-equivalent.
finalStateSpec:
module TS2PANT_L2_TYPED_MIRROR.

> ════════════════════════════════
> Single-milestone workstream: L2 as a typed mirror of OpaqueExpr.
> ════════════════════════════════
> After this gameplan, L1 has comb-typed / forall / exists
> mirroring L2's CombTyped / Forall / Exists. The μ-search
> recognizer produces L1 comb-typed directly at the
> TS-AST → L1 boundary; lowerL1MuSearch and
> isCanonicalMuSearchForm are removed. L1→L2 is now a pure
> 1:1 mechanical mapping. Documentation in the IR doc + 
> AGENTS.md + imperative-IR workstream doc articulates the
> honest layering principle: L1 absorbs Pant-target forms;
> L1→L2 carries no recognition; L2 is a typed mirror, not
> a transformation IR.

IR1Form.
L2Form.
Walker.
FreeVarSet.
RecognitionSite.
IR1Shape.
SnapshotState.
Document.
DocumentKind.
comb-typed => IR1Form.
forall => IR1Form.
exists => IR1Form.
comb-typed-l2 => L2Form.
forall-l2 => L2Form.
exists-l2 => L2Form.
ts-ast-to-l1 => RecognitionSite.
l1-to-l2 => RecognitionSite.
comb-typed-l1 => IR1Shape.
block-let-while => IR1Shape.
identical => SnapshotState.
drifted => SnapshotState.
ir-doc => DocumentKind.
agents-md => DocumentKind.
imperative-ir-doc => DocumentKind.

has-arm-for? w: Walker, f: IR1Form => Bool.
scopes-binder? f: IR1Form, vars: FreeVarSet => Bool.
lowers-to? l1: IR1Form, l2: L2Form => Bool.
respects-capture-risk? f: IR1Form => Bool.
halts-at-shadow? f: IR1Form => Bool.
recognizer-fires-at? site: RecognitionSite => Bool.
post-recognizer-shape => IR1Shape.
snapshot-state => SnapshotState.
lower-l1-musearch-exists? => Bool.
is-canonical-musearch-form-exists? => Bool.
document-kind d: Document => DocumentKind.
mentions-layering-principle? d: Document => Bool.
mentions-no-l1-l2-recognition? d: Document => Bool.
mentions-l2-not-substitution-target? d: Document => Bool.
---

> Vocabulary contract.
all f: IR1Form, w: Walker | has-arm-for? w f.
all f: IR1Form, vars: FreeVarSet | scopes-binder? f vars.
all f: IR1Form | respects-capture-risk? f.
all f: IR1Form | halts-at-shadow? f.
lowers-to? comb-typed comb-typed-l2.
lowers-to? forall forall-l2.
lowers-to? exists exists-l2.

> Recognition has moved upstream.
recognizer-fires-at? ts-ast-to-l1.
~(recognizer-fires-at? l1-to-l2).
post-recognizer-shape = comb-typed-l1.
~(lower-l1-musearch-exists?).
~(is-canonical-musearch-form-exists?).

> Snapshot equivalence on every μ-search fixture.
snapshot-state = identical.

> Documentation invariants.
all d: Document |
  document-kind d = ir-doc ->
    mentions-layering-principle? d and
    mentions-no-l1-l2-recognition? d and
    mentions-l2-not-substitution-target? d.

all d: Document |
  document-kind d = agents-md ->
    mentions-layering-principle? d.

all d: Document |
  document-kind d = imperative-ir-doc ->
    mentions-layering-principle? d.
